raw_consumer_statement: In XX/XX/year> I was contacted by a man claiming to be with XXXX XXXX and selling a stay at the hotel. I provided information including my credit card and driver 's license, being told I would not be charged until I agreed to everything. The man sent me an email from a suspicious email address, using a different company name, and terms which I did not like. At that time I told him I did not want to go through with the purchase and he was not authorized to charge my card ( this conversation was recorded ). He charged my credit card anyway. I disputed this, but Amex ruled against the dispute because the fraudster provided an unsigned paper receipt, and nothing more. Amex refused to listen to the recording or consider my evidence. I disputed this against, and the fraudster provided the same receipt, but with my signature forged. Amex did not provide an explanation or allow me to comment, but ruled in the fraudsters ' favor. At this time Amex was unable to provide a company name or address for the fraudster. I submitted a different kind of dispute because I had never received any kind of service for the fraudulent charge. Amex declined to review any of my evidence or listen to the recording. In XXXX this year, Amex ruled in the fraudsters ' favor, not providing me any evidence. Amex continues to be unable to provide a business name or address for this fraudster, has not provided the most recent evidence the fraudster provided, and refuses to look at my evidence. Amex acknowledged on the phone the account and charge looks like fraud, but has continued to allow this fraudster to charge Amex Cards and has continued to facilitate this fraud.
